prompt:
Work the next open Phase B implementation ticket using a fresh sub-agent.

Orchestrator rules (this chat stays thin):
1. Open docs/phases/phase-B.md and find the first ticket heading that is still "- [ ]" (not "- [x]").
2. Do NOT implement the ticket in this parent context. Launch exactly one Task subagent (generalPurpose) with a clean, self-contained brief for ONLY that ticket.
3. Subagent brief must include: ticket id/title/Done when/Notes; repo path /Users/mark/Development/Personal/tech-support; follow docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-18-tech-support-gba-design.md section 6 (Phase B rules); Sips is art/UX only (no fork); controls A=retry/confirm/hold-fix Select=notepad B=title on end screen Start=reserved; failure only at shift end (never mid-shift); completion % = fixed/(fixed+failed), pass threshold default 75; run make with DEVKITPRO=/opt/devkitpro; emulator is mgba; mark "- [x]" only if Done when met; on blocker leave unchecked and add BLOCKED under Notes; return a short summary (id, changes, build status, blocked?).
4. Wait for the subagent. Do not start a second ticket.
5. If subagent succeeded, confirm the checkbox and build; if blocked, stop advancing and do not commit.
6. After a successful ticket: create ONE logical git commit for that ticket only (message focuses on why; include code + phase-B.md checkbox; do not commit unrelated files). Do not push unless the user asked to push.
7. If no open Phase B tickets remain, do not start Phase C — report Phase B complete.
8. End the tick with: ticket id, what changed, build status, commit hash (or none if blocked), next open ticket id.
9. Keep parent replies short.
